args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(a:Asset)
RETURN aas.idShort, aas.id, a.globalAssetId, a.assetType
ORDER BY aas.idShort

MATCH (container:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

MATCH (container:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN e.idShort, asset.globalAssetId

// List all submodels of Hall4 with their semanticIds
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

// List submodels of MiR100_001 to confirm it's an AGV/transport robot
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:mir100:001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

// Traverse the HierarchicalStructures in Hall4 to find contained assets
MATCH (container:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
MATCH (e)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
MATCH (e)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*0..]->(child:Entity)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(childAsset:Asset)
RETURN e.idShort AS parent, child.idShort AS child, asset.globalAssetId AS parentId, childAsset.globalAssetId AS childAssetId

// Walk the entity tree in HierarchicalStructures of Hall4
MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*1..]->(e:Entity)
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN e.idShort, e.entityType, asset.globalAssetId
ORDER BY e.idShort

// Check submodels of MiR100_001 to confirm it's an AGV
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:mir100:001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
WHERE sm.idShort <> 'ServiceRequestNotification'
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

// Walk from MiR100_001 instance to its type shell
MATCH (instance:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:mir100:001'})
      -[:DERIVED_FROM]->(type:AssetAdministrationShell)
RETURN type.id, type.idShort

// Check submodels of the MiR100 type shell
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:mir100:type'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

## What to Do — Immediate Steps

Based on the MiR100 documentation:

### 1. Check the Safety Zone
According to the MiR100 manual (Section 2.4 — Safety Circuit):
- The red LED and emergency stop is triggered when a person or object enters the safety zones where the planner cannot respond in time
- **If the person/object has left the safety zone, the emergency stop should reset automatically**
- Wait a moment to see if it clears on its own

### 2. If It Doesn't Reset Automatically
The manual states:
> "If any internal errors in the safety circuit occur, the robot will immediately go into emergency stop which means all moving parts will be voltage free by mechanical disconnection. Only when the redundancy is provided, it is possible to reset the emergency stop and continue."

This indicates a **persistent internal safety circuit fault** that requires:
- Technical service contact
- The redundant SICK Safety components need inspection

### 3. Contact Support
- MiR Support: support@mir-robots.com or +45 20 377 577
- Or contact your retailer via: http://mobile-industrial-robots.com/en/retailers/

### 4. Diagnostic Check (if accessible)
- Log into the MiR Web Interface for the robot
- Navigate to: **Service > Status > Diagnostics**
- Check that all components show green/OK status
- This will help identify if the issue is with specific safety components

---

**Safety Note:** Do not attempt to manually override the emergency stop. The system is designed with mechanical disconnection of all moving parts for your safety. Wait for technical service to restore the redundant safety circuit before restarting the robot.
